framework.name: Capability Coherence System (CCS)
framework.description: Formal mapping from hardware MESI cache coherence states to authorization states for multi-agent delegation. Treats revocation as a coherence problem rather than a latency problem, with transient states (EIA, MIC) representing the actual damage window where unauthorized ops accumulate.
framework.raw_quote: authorisation revocation in multi-agent delegation chains is operationally equivalent to cache coherence in shared-memory multiprocessors under bounded-staleness semantics

source.title: [PDF] Structural Equivalence Between Memory Consistency Models ... - arXiv
